reference.title: Validation Settings
reference.shortdesc: Configure validation engine, automatic validation, cross-reference checking, DITA rules, profiling validation, severity overrides, custom rules, and large file optimization.

section.title: ditacraft.validationEngine
section.p: Type: String (enum)

dlentry.dd: Lightweight validation with content model checking. Fast and suitable for most DITA authoring workflows. The default engine.
Full DTD validation using TypesXML parser. Provides 100% W3C conformance with complete DTD support. Best for strict compliance checking.
External validation using libxml2's xmllint tool. Requires xmllint to be installed on your system. Provides excellent performance and accuracy.

section.title: ditacraft.autoValidate
section.p: Type: Boolean
When enabled, DitaCraft validates DITA files automatically:

ul.li: After typing stops, with smart debouncing (300ms for topics, 1000ms for maps)

section.p: Set to false to disable automatic validation. You can still validate manually using Ctrl+Shift+V.

ul.li: Working with very large files that cause performance issues
Editing files that are intentionally incomplete
When you prefer manual validation control

section.title: ditacraft.maxNumberOfProblems
section.p: Type: Number
The maximum number of diagnostic problems reported per file. Increase this value if you work with very large files and need to see all issues. Decrease it if the Problems panel becomes overwhelming.

section.title: ditacraft.ditaRulesEnabled
section.p: Type: Boolean

Enable or disable the DITA Rules Engine. When enabled, DitaCraft validates your content against 35 Schematron-equivalent rules covering specification compliance, best practices, and accessibility (including 10 DITA 2.0-specific rules). Set to false to disable all DITA rules validation.

section.title: ditacraft.ditaRulesCategories
section.p: Type: Array of strings
Select which DITA rule categories to apply. Available categories:

ul.li: "mandatory" — Rules required by the DITA specification
"recommendation" — Best practices recommended by the DITA specification

section.p: For example, to run only mandatory and accessibility rules:

section.title: ditacraft.crossRefValidationEnabled
section.p: Type: Boolean

Enable or disable cross-reference validation. When enabled, DitaCraft checks that href, conref, keyref, and conkeyref attributes point to valid targets. Set to false if you are working with incomplete content where references are expected to be unresolved.

section.title: ditacraft.subjectSchemeValidationEnabled
section.p: Type: Boolean

Enable or disable subject scheme validation. When enabled and a subject scheme map is present in the workspace, DitaCraft validates that profiling attribute values (audience, platform, product, etc.) match the controlled vocabulary defined by the scheme. Set to false to allow any profiling attribute values.

section.title: ditacraft.ditaVersion
section.p: Type: String (enum)

Override the auto-detected DITA version for validation. When set to "auto" (default), DitaCraft detects the version from @DITAArchVersion or DOCTYPE. Set an explicit version to force version-specific rules (e.g., "2.0" to enable DITA 2.0 rules for all documents).

section.title: ditacraft.schemaFormat
section.p: Type: String (enum)
Options: "dtd", "rng"
Schema format for validation. The default "dtd" uses standard DTD validation. Set to "rng" to enable RelaxNG schema validation using salve-annos. Requires ditacraft.rngSchemaPath to be set.

section.title: ditacraft.rngSchemaPath
section.p: Type: String

Path to the directory containing RNG schema files (e.g., a DITA-OT's schema directory). Required when ditacraft.schemaFormat is set to "rng". RNG schemas are compiled to JSON and cached for performance (up to 20 grammars).

section.title: ditacraft.rootMap
section.p: Type: String
Explicit root map path (relative to workspace root). Leave empty for automatic root map discovery. Set via the DITA: Set Root Map command or directly in settings.
The root map determines the key space for keyref/conkeyref resolution, cross-reference validation scope, and subject scheme discovery. See for details.

section.title: Rate Limiting
section.p: DitaCraft includes built-in rate limiting to prevent performance issues:

ul.li: Limit: 10 validation requests per second per file
Behavior: Excess requests are silently skipped during auto-validation
Manual Validation: Shows a warning if rate limited

section.p: Rate limiting cannot be configured but ensures stable performance during rapid editing.

section.title: ditacraft.validationSeverityOverrides
section.p: Type: Object
Override the severity of any diagnostic code. Map diagnostic codes to "error", "warning", "information", "hint", or "off" (to suppress entirely).

section.title: ditacraft.customRulesFile
section.p: Type: String

Absolute path to a JSON file defining custom regex validation rules. Each rule specifies a regex pattern, message, severity, and optional file type filter. Rules are re-loaded automatically when the file changes.

section.title: ditacraft.largeFileThresholdKB
section.p: Type: Number

Files larger than this threshold (in KB) skip heavy validation phases (cross-references, DITA rules, profiling, custom rules, etc.) for performance. Set to 0 to disable the optimization and always run all phases. A DITA-PERF-001 informational diagnostic is shown when phases are skipped.

section.title: Comment-Based Rule Suppression
section.p: You can suppress specific diagnostic codes inline using XML comments:

ul.li: <!-- ditacraft-disable CODE --> — Suppress a code from this point until re-enabled
<!-- ditacraft-enable CODE --> — Re-enable a previously suppressed code
<!-- ditacraft-disable-file CODE --> — Suppress a code for the entire file

section.p: Multiple codes can be specified, separated by spaces: <!-- ditacraft-disable DITA-SCH-001 DITA-ID-002 -->

section.title: Setting Up xmllint
section.p: To use the xmllint engine, install libxml2:

ol.li: Add the bin directory to your PATH
